title: Breaking the Fast of Ramadan Without Excuse
subtitle: Violating the Sacred Month Allah Made Obligatory

verses.arabic: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا كُتِبَ عَلَيْكُمُ الصِّيَامُ كَمَا كُتِبَ عَلَى الَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ
verses.translation: O you who have believed, decreed upon you is fasting as it was decreed upon those before you that you may become righteous.

verses.arabic: شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ الَّذِي أُنزِلَ فِيهِ الْقُرْآنُ هُدًى لِّلنَّاسِ وَبَيِّنَاتٍ مِّنَ الْهُدَىٰ وَالْفُرْقَانِ ۚ فَمَن شَهِدَ مِنكُمُ الشَّهْرَ فَلْيَصُمْهُ
verses.translation: The month of Ramadan [is that] in which was revealed the Quran, a guidance for the people and clear proofs of guidance and criterion. So whoever sights [the crescent of] the month, let him fast it.

verses.arabic: إِنَّ عِدَّةَ الشُّهُورِ عِندَ اللَّهِ اثْنَا عَشَرَ شَهْرًا فِي كِتَابِ اللَّهِ يَوْمَ خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ مِنْهَا أَرْبَعَةٌ حُرُمٌ
verses.translation: Indeed, the number of months with Allah is twelve [lunar] months in the register of Allah [from] the day He created the heavens and the earth; of these, four are sacred.

brief_explanation: Allah made fasting in Ramadan a clear obligation upon every believing man and woman who is able, decreeing it as He decreed it upon the nations before — making it a pillar of the faith. Breaking this fast without a legitimate excuse (illness, travel, pregnancy, or similar) is a direct violation of an explicit Quranic command and a desecration of the most sacred month of the year. Scholars of hadith report that the Prophet (peace be upon him) said one who breaks a day of Ramadan without excuse cannot make it up fully even if they fast every day for the rest of their life — indicating the gravity of this violation. It represents a wilful rejection of a divine obligation and an act of ingratitude toward Allah for the blessing of this month.
capital_punishment: There is no hadd execution specifically prescribed for breaking the Ramadan fast without excuse. The scholars agree it is a kabira (major sin) requiring sincere tawbah, making up the missed day (qada), and in certain cases — such as breaking the fast through sexual intercourse — also expiation (kaffarah): freeing a slave, or fasting sixty consecutive days, or feeding sixty poor people. The kaffarah ruling is agreed upon by all four madhabs for the specific case of intercourse during the fasting day of Ramadan.
